data.title: Northern Trust GAAP EPS of $2.42 beats by $0.07, revenue of $2.14B beats by $80M
data.description:
• Northern Trust press release (NTRS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.42 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $2.14B (+8.6% Y/Y) beats by $80M.

data.title: Greenwich LifeSciences surges as FDA clears commercially manufactured product candidate
data.description:
Shares of Greenwich LifeSciences (GLSI) climbed ~33% in the premarket on Thursday after the cancer drug developer said that the U.S. FDA has approved the first commercially manufactured lot of its product candidate GP2 for use in a late-stage trial.

The Stafford, Texas-based biotech is currently advancing its Phase 3 trial, FLAMINGO-01, to evaluate its breast cancer therapy, GLSI-100, a combination of GP2 and a cytokine known as GM-CSF that helps stimulate the production of white blood cells.

The company recently submitted data on the first three commercial lots of the GP2 active ingredient, which it manufactured in 2023, to the regulator. Greenwich (GLSI) said that following a review, the regulator has now approved the first lot of GP2 vials for use in FLAMINGO-01, which evaluates GLSI-100 against breast cancer recurrence in the U.S.

The company also shared FLAMINGO-01 open-label Phase 3 data on Thursday, noting that after a series of GLSI-100 injections, those in the 250-patient non-HLA-A*02 arm have shown a nearly 80% reduction in recurrence rate.

“We plan to start using these new GP2 vials in the coming weeks at all 40 US sites,” said CEO Snehal Patel, adding, “We look forward to submitting the same manufacturing data to regulatory agencies in Europe, the United Kingdom, and Canada.”

The company stated that, in addition to Phase 3 data, results from commercial manufacturing of these three lots will be crucial for filing a marketing application for GLSI-100 with the FDA and overseas regulators.

data.title: Mobileye Global beats top-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Mobileye Global press release (MBLY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 in-line.
  • Revenue of $446M (-9.0% Y/Y) beats by $14.15M.
  • Operating cash flow for the year ended December 27, 2025 was $602 million. Cash used in purchases of property and equipment was $79 million for that same period.
  • Average System Price was $50.8 in the fourth quarter of 2025, compared to $50.0 in the prior year period primarily due to a higher percentage of SuperVision-related revenue as compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Full-year 2026 financial guidance implies flat to 5% year-over-year revenue growth, including approximately 19% year-over-year revenue growth expected in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Old Republic Non-GAAP EPS of $0.74 misses by $0.14, revenue of $2.36B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Old Republic press release (ORI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.74 misses by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $2.36B (+9.3% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Consolidated net premiums and fees earned of $2.1 billion, an increase of 9.8% over last year.
  • Net investment income of $183.8 million, an increase of 7.9% over last year.
  • Consolidated combined ratio of 96.0%, compared to 92.7% last year.

data.title: Activist investor Engaged Capital boosts its stake in BRC
data.description:
Engaged Capital boosted its activist stake in BRC Inc. (BRCC) to 12.7% after purchasing more shares. In an SEC filing, BRC (BRCC) disclosed that Engaged Capital now holds 14,597,791 shares.

Recent institutional ownership data indicated that Engaged Capital is the most heavily invested institutional holder in BRC (BRCC) by dollar value. Engaged Capital also has stakes in Cognex (CGNX), Portillo's (PTLO), Evolent Health (EVH), VF Corporation (VFC), and Yeti Holdings (YETI).

BRC (BRCC) issued preliminary guidance at the ICR Conference earlier in the month. For FY25, BRC (BRCC) said it expects to report at least $395 million in revenue, a gross margin rate of at least 35%, and adjusted EBITDA of at least $20 million. Those results were noted to meet the previously issued guidance. The company is not scheduled to report full earnings until near the end of March. 

Shares of BRC (BRCC) are down 10.4% for the early part of 2026.

data.title: Vonage and Lyft’s Freenow launch silent authentication API in U.S.
data.description:
• Vonage, part of Ericsson (ERIC), and Freenow by Lyft (LYFT) expanded their partnership in the U.S. with the launch of Vonage’s Silent Authentication API to enhance user verification and reduce fraud.
  • “Launching Vonage Silent Authentication API for the U.S. customers marks a transformative milestone for Freenow by Lyft, the European taxi app serving nine markets and over 180 cities," said Tim Ossenfort, EVP Technology & Data at Freenow by Lyft.
  • Freenow uses Vonage Network APIs to enable SIM swap detection and silent authentication capabilities to invisibly verify user identities and prevent account takeovers in real time.

data.title: Tilray Medical expands in Italy with Tilray Medical Italia launch
data.description:
• Tilray Medical, a division of Tilray Brands (TLRY), announced a strategic update to its medical cannabis operations in Italy with the launch of Tilray Medical Italia, the new name of the entity formerly known as FL Group.
  • The move to Tilray Medical Italia underscores the company’s effort to build a unified medical cannabis platform across Europe while strengthening its presence in one of the region’s key regulated markets.
  • Tilray Medical Italia is expanding its footprint in Italy through a growing portfolio of medical cannabis products authorized by the Italian Ministry of Health and supported by a partnership with Molteni Farmaceutici.

data.title: Axogen stock dips after pricing $124M offering
data.description:
• Axogen (AXGN) shares fell over 7% in premarket trade on Thursday after the surgical solutions developer priced an upsized public offering of 4M shares at $31.00 per share.
  • In addition, Axogen has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600K shares at the public offering price.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to be around $124M, assuming no exercise of the underwriters’ option to purchase additional shares. Axogen intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for early payoff and termination of its term loan facility with Oberland Capital, working capital, capital expenditures, and other general corporate purposes.
  • The proposed offering is expected to close on January 23, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • AXGN --7.3% premarket to $30.59
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Atlantic Union Bank Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.11, revenue of $387.1M beats by $8.15M
data.description:
• Atlantic Union Bank press release (AUB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $387.1M (+77.2% Y/Y) beats by $8.15M.
  • At December 31, 2025, the ACL was $321.3 million, an increase of $1.3 million from the prior quarter, comprised of an allowance for loan and lease losses (“ALLL”) of $295.1 million and a reserve for unfunded commitments (“RUC”) of $26.2 million. This increase in the ACL was primarily driven by loan growth in the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: Target declares $1.14 dividend
data.description:
• Target (TGT) declares $1.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.3%
  • Payable March 1; for shareholders of record Feb. 11; ex-div Feb. 11.
  • See TGT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
